key_insight: Spacer-layer Cl doping can tune low-n square-planar nickelates to the same nominal Ni valence and correlation regime as known superconducting higher-n compounds, making n=2 and 3 candidates for superconductivity.
engineering_relevance: This offers a chemically practical design route to engineer nickelate superconductors without heavily altering the NiO2 planes, potentially improving synthesis feasibility for lower-n materials.
method: DFT+DMFT calculations on La-based low-n square-planar nickelates
Model spacer-layer Cl substitution to compensate charge and tune Ni valence
Compare layer-resolved Ni-d electronic structure and correlation strength against superconducting higher-n nickelates
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that electron-compensated n=2 and n=3 compounds show Ni-d correlations comparable to superconducting higher-n members while retaining the low-energy Ni-d electronic structure.
watch_item: The prediction is computational and depends on whether spacer-layer Cl doping can be synthesized stably without introducing disorder that suppresses superconductivity.
paper_title: Layer-resolved Electronic Structure and Correlation of Low-$n$ Square-planar Nickelates: A DFT+DMFT Prediction of Superconducting Candidates
